title: Strategy & Ops Specialist
description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Streamline operations and foster a culture of well-being to enable teams to thrive while delivering high-impact outcomes.

  • Oversee operational tracking, performance monitoring, and reporting of key initiatives to ensure transparency and accountability.

  • Manage administrative and logistical functions to support the smooth execution of projects and initiatives.

  • Support the planning, coordination, and delivery of training and skills development programs for internal teams.

  • Develop clear, engaging technical documentation and reports to support ongoing initiatives.

  • Coordinate outreach efforts to strengthen internal and external community engagement and organizational presence.

  • Manage ad hoc projects and special assignments, ensuring timely and high-quality delivery.

  • Lead employee engagement initiatives, including recognition programs, feedback mechanisms, and team-building activities.

Requirements & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field.

  • 3–7 years of experience in operations, strategy, or program management.

  • Strong understanding of business operations and process improvement methodologies.

  • Demonstrated experience in implementing and optimizing processes to improve operational efficiency.

  • Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail in tracking, reporting, and documentation.

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to adapt to diverse audiences.

  • Proven ability to collaborate and build relationships across teams and stakeholders.

  • Proficiency in data analysis, trend identification, and driving operational improvements.

  • Experience in event planning and execution, including logistics and engagement.

  • Proficiency with productivity and project management tools (e.g., Microsoft Office, Google Workspace).

  • Familiarity with AI and automation technologies.


    If you believe you fit the requirements for the role, please submit your application below or drop us an email directly quoting the job title.

    Due to an anticipated high volume of applicants, we regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. The information provided is for recruitment purposes only.

    Know someone who would be a great fit for this role? Refer them to us and get rewarded.

    Cornerstone Global Partners (EA License Number: 19C9859) is an affirmative equal-opportunity employer and recruitment firm. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, colour, religion, creed, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class.

    Eugene Then
    eugene.then@cornerstoneglobalpartners.com

    EA Registration Number: R22104742.

    Cornerstone Global Partners Pte Ltd (EA License: 19C9859)

postedCompany.description:
Cornerstone Global Partners is an Asia-centric recruitment solutions firm with a global reach through offices in China, Hong Kong, Singapore and the United States, along with network offices in 40 other countries around the globe. We provide services which include traditional C-level to mid-level executive search, contract staffing, market entry/ outbound investment consultancy and organizational design solutions through our industry-specialized recruiting team. 

Cornerstone’s leadership team is comprised of a diverse group of thought leaders in their respective industry practices. The team has demonstrated expertise in finding the right solutions for the unique challenges of the Asian market with the completion of over 1000 placements in the region. Cornerstone’s Partners and Consultants are industry-specialized and have a demonstrated track record of performance and success.
